description: TUI polish (fuzzy selectors, color, completions), cross-compilation release packaging with a trebup installer, and a parasitic CI workflow that tracks foundry upstream releases automatically.

userStories.title: Interactive Fuzzy Deployment Selector Infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable fuzzy-search selector for deployments and a shared confirmation prompt abstraction so that commands can fall back to interactive selection when no argument is provided.
acceptanceCriteria: `nucleo-matcher`, `dialoguer`, and `console` crates added to workspace `Cargo.toml`
`crates/treb-cli/src/ui/mod.rs` created with `pub mod selector;` and `pub mod prompt;`, registered in `lib.rs`
`fuzzy_select_deployment` returns `Ok(None)` for an empty deployment list
`fuzzy_select_deployment` returns `Err(TrebError::Cli(...))` in non-TTY mode when no query is supplied
`ui::prompt::confirm(message, default)` returns `default` in non-TTY environments
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Wire Deployment Selector into Commands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `show`, `verify`, and `tag` commands to accept an optional deployment argument and fall back to the fuzzy selector when omitted, so that I can run these commands without remembering deployment IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: `ShowArgs.deployment` changed to `Option<String>`; `fuzzy_select_deployment_id` called when `None`
`VerifyArgs.deployment` changed to `Option<String>`; selector called when `None` and `--all` is not set
`TagArgs.deployment` changed to `Option<String>`; selector called when `None`
`prune.rs` and `reset.rs` use `ui::prompt::confirm()` in place of any ad-hoc confirmation code
`treb show` with no argument in a non-TTY environment exits with a clear error message, not a panic
Explicit deployment ID arguments bypass the selector entirely
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Interactive Network Selector and Batch Multiselect
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a fuzzy network selector for `treb run` and a multiselect for `treb verify --all` so that I can pick networks and deployments interactively without typing exact names.
acceptanceCriteria: `fuzzy_select_network(networks)` added to `ui/selector.rs`; returns `Ok(None)` for empty list and `Err` in non-TTY
`multiselect_deployments(deployments, prompt)` added to `ui/selector.rs`; returns `Err` in non-TTY
`run.rs` calls `fuzzy_select_network()` when `--network` is not provided and a TTY is detected
`verify --all` uses `multiselect_deployments` for interactive subset selection in TTY mode; verifies all in non-TTY
`fuzzy_select_network` with an empty list returns `Ok(None)` (unit test)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Colored Output Audit and NO_COLOR Support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all color output to respect the `NO_COLOR` environment variable and `--no-color` flag so that the tool works cleanly in CI and colorblind-friendly environments.
acceptanceCriteria: `owo-colors = { version = "4", features = ["supports-colors"] }` added to workspace `Cargo.toml`
`crates/treb-cli/src/ui/color.rs` created with `should_use_color()`, `color_enabled()`, and palette constants (`STAGE`, `SUCCESS`, `WARNING`, `ERROR`, `MUTED`)
`--no-color` global flag added to `Cli` struct in `main.rs`; calls `owo_colors::control::set_should_colorize(false)` when set
`should_use_color()` returns `false` when `NO_COLOR` env var is set to any value (unit test)
`should_use_color()` returns `false` when `TERM=dumb` (unit test)
Color palette constants compile as `Send + Sync` (compile-time check)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Shell Completions and Long-Form Help Text
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `treb completions <shell>` to generate shell completion scripts and all commands to have long-form `--help` descriptions so that the tool is discoverable and self-documenting.
acceptanceCriteria: `clap_complete = "4"` added as a build-dependency in `crates/treb-cli/Cargo.toml`
`crates/treb-cli/build.rs` emits completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, and elvish into `$OUT_DIR/completions/`
`treb completions <shell>` subcommand added; generates completions to stdout at runtime

Long-form `#[command(long_about)]` or `///` doc comments added for all commands listed in the PRD (run, show, list, verify, tag, register, sync, fork subcommands, prune, reset, migrate, init, config, gen deploy, compose)
`treb completions bash` exits 0 and stdout contains `treb` (integration test)
`treb completions fish` exits 0 and stdout contains `complete -c treb` (integration test)
`treb run --help` stdout contains the substring `"dry-run"` (integration test)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Cross-Compilation CI Matrix
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow that builds static release binaries for linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, macos-x86_64, and macos-aarch64 on every version tag so that distributable artifacts are produced automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/release-build.yml` created with a 4-target matrix (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, aarch64-unknown-linux-musl, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin)
Linux targets use `cross` (Docker-based); macOS targets use `rustup target add` without Docker
Each target produces a `.tar.gz` archive and a `.sha256` checksum file
Both files uploaded as workflow artifacts via `actions/upload-artifact@v4`
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` created with check, test, clippy (`-D warnings`), and fmt jobs using `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2`
Release build workflow triggers only on `v*.*.*` tags, not on every push
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Release Packaging and `trebup` Installer Script
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install treb with a single `curl | sh` command so that I can get the correct static binary for my platform without manually downloading from GitHub Releases.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/release.yml` created; triggers on `v*.*.*` tags after `release-build.yml` completes; creates a GitHub Release with all 4 `.tar.gz` and `.sha256` files attached
`scripts/trebup` POSIX shell script created and committed with executable bit
Script maps `uname -s` / `uname -m` to the correct target for all 4 supported platforms; exits with `"Unsupported platform"` for unknown platforms
Script verifies SHA-256 checksum before writing the binary to disk; exits non-zero on mismatch without installing
`TREB_INSTALL_DIR` env override respected for custom install path
`TREB_VERSION` env override respected for installing a specific version
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Parasitic CI for Foundry Version Tracking
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow that detects new foundry releases and either auto-merges a clean version bump or opens a GitHub Issue with build errors so that upstream breaking changes are surfaced automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/foundry-track.yml` created with `cron: '0 6 * * *'` and `workflow_dispatch` triggers
Workflow reads `.foundry-pin` at repo root and compares to latest foundry release tag; skips if already up to date
Bump branch named `foundry-bump/<tag>` is created and pushed on version change
`cargo check --workspace` and `cargo test --workspace` run on the bump branch with `continue-on-error: true`
On failure: GitHub Issue opened with build output; duplicate detection via `gh issue list --search` skips creation if matching open issue exists
On success: PR opened with auto-merge enabled using `gh pr merge --auto --squash`
`.foundry-pin` file created at repo root containing the current pinned foundry commit SHA
Workflow `permissions:` block declares `contents: write`, `pull-requests: write`, `issues: write`
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)

userStories.title: Final End-to-End Integration Test Suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a comprehensive end-to-end integration test suite that exercises full multi-command workflows using in-process Anvil so that all phases are confirmed to compose correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: `crates/treb-cli/tests/e2e_workflow.rs` created with `setup_project` helper that copies the counter fixture, spawns Anvil on port 0, and runs `treb init`
init → run → list workflow test: exit 0, `treb list --json` returns one deployment entry with a non-zero address
run → show workflow test: `treb show <id> --json` output contains `address`, `contract_name`, `chain_id`
run → tag → list-with-tag-filter test: `treb list --tag v1.0.0 --json` returns exactly one result
run → prune --dry-run on clean registry test: exit 0, stdout contains `"Nothing to prune"`
run → reset → list test: `treb list --json` returns empty array after reset
`treb list --no-color` stdout contains no `\x1b[` ANSI escape sequences
All existing tests continue to pass (`cargo test --workspace`)
Typecheck passes (`cargo check --workspace`)
